title: Benchmark finite field linear algebra against fflas-ffpack
description:
Rigorous performance benchmarking of gf2 finite field linear algebra against fflas-ffpack. **This is a mandatory completion gate for the linear algebra epic.**

## Benchmark suite

| Operation | Sizes |
|-----------|-------|
| Matrix multiply (`fgemm`) | 64x64, 256x256, 1024x1024, 4096x4096 |
| Gaussian elimination | 64x64, 256x256, 1024x1024, 4096x4096 |
| Matrix inversion | 64x64, 256x256, 1024x1024 |
| Linear solve (`Ax = b`) | 64x64, 256x256, 1024x1024, 4096x4096 |

## Field coverage

- Small GF(p): p = 7, 31, 251, 65521
- Medium GF(p): p = 2^31 - 1 (Mersenne), large 32-bit primes
- GF(2^m): m = 8, 16, 32

## Targets

- **GF(p)**: Within 2x of fflas-ffpack throughput
- **GF(2^m)**: **Exceed** fflas-ffpack (we have direct PCLMULQDQ; they route through float BLAS)

## Methodology

- Criterion.rs benchmarks with statistical rigor
- fflas-ffpack built with OpenBLAS, `-O3 -march=native`
- Same hardware, same matrix data, median of 100+ iterations
- Published results with full methodology documentation

## Dependencies

- Matrix multiplication kernels (d48a3cfd)
- Gaussian elimination (c3f8c1cb)
